yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday's 60.3-mile long ride delivered 136 TSS at an excellent controlled HR of 124 bpm, showing strong aerobic efficiency and discipline. This was the week's anchor session and executed perfectly. The distance and TSS align well with race prep needs 35 days out from IM 70.3 Oregon.
todayRecommendation: Complete today's planned run + open water swim to close out the peak volume week. Keep the run easy given the 10.2-mile effort on Tuesday and yesterday's 60-mile ride — focus on form and hip comfort, not pace. The open water swim is valuable race-specific practice.
weekProgress: Week 8 of 10 sits at 448 TSS of 500 target (90% complete) with only today remaining. The major sessions are complete: Tuesday's 10.2-mile long run (102 TSS), Wednesday's brick, and Saturday's 60-mile ride (136 TSS). Today's run + OWS should bring the week home near target, making this the successful peak volume week as planned.
keyInsight: Last night's sleep score of 59 with only 24 minutes of REM following yesterday's hard 60-mile ride signals incomplete recovery heading into today's final session.
